prevalence.source: Orphanet; European metabolic registries and newborn screening program summaries
prevalence.trend: stable; earlier diagnosis supported by broader newborn screening and metabolic referral networks

incidence.source: European inborn errors of metabolism incidence syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high per-patient morbidity; CKD and neurodevelopmental complications
burden.mortalityRate: Low with early treatment; higher in severe neonatal-onset without rapid care

vulnerablePopulations: patients with delayed transition to adult metabolic care
patients with chronic kidney disease from MMA
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Newborn screening/MS/MS with confirmatory methylmalonic acid quantification, acylcarnitine profile (C3), genetic confirmation; classify B12-responsive forms

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: moderate to high depending on country; confirmatory testing and referral networks widely available
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Good with early diagnosis, diet/medication adherence, and specialist follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Newborn screening in many countries; metabolic centers provide confirmatory diagnostics and long-term care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Emergency metabolic protocols, medical nutrition and protein restriction, carnitine, B12 for responsive types, renal and neurodevelopmental surveillance; transplant evaluation for select severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Transition-to-adult care and long-term adherence; cross-country variation in screening panels
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong metabolic networks and access to specialized formulas and multidisciplinary follow-up
